question: [File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: listStakingRewardsByAccountId()] Can an unprivileged external user supply an alias string that matches multiple alive entities (triggering the multipleAliasMatch error path in EntityService) to cause an unhandled exception that leaks internal state about which accounts share an alias, revealing account IDs that have accumulated staking rewards?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: listStakingRewardsByAccountId()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that isValidAccount() uses entityExistenceQuery which only checks the entity table (not entity_history), allowing queries for accounts that were deleted and re-created to return reward records from the previous account lifecycle?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: extractStakingRewardsQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a timestamp filter with a value of exactly the current consensus timestamp to retrieve in-flight reward transfers that have been written to the DB but not yet finalized, leaking pending reward amounts before they are reflected in the account's pending_reward field?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: listStakingRewardsByAccountId()] Can an unprivileged external user enumerate all accounts that have ever received staking rewards by iterating account IDs from 0.0.1 upward and checking whether the rewards array is non-empty, building a complete map of staking participants and their reward histories without any access control?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: listStakingRewardsByAccountId()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of any check on the decline_reward flag in listStakingRewardsByAccountId() to query reward records for accounts that have set decline_reward=true, confirming whether the flag was honored by the network or whether rewards were paid despite the opt-out?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: extractStakingRewardsQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a timestamp=gte:X&timestamp=lte:X (same value for both bounds) to create a zero-width time window that, due to how updateConditionsAndParamsWithInValues() handles range conditions, either returns unexpected records or causes a SQL error that leaks query structure?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: listStakingRewardsByAccountId()] Can an unprivileged external user use the reward amount and timestamp data returned by listStakingRewardsByAccountId() to calculate the exact staked balance and node assignment of a target account at each reward period, effectively reconstructing the account's complete staking history without any authorization?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: listStakingRewardsByAccountId()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a realm.alias format (e.g., 0.AAAQEAYEAUDAOCAJCAIREEYUCULBOGAZ) to getEncodedId() where the realm component is mismatched, causing the alias lookup to fail and fall through to an unexpected code path that returns reward data for a default account?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: listStakingRewardsByAccountId()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that StakingRewardTransferService.listStakingRewardsByAccountIdQuery does not include a payer_account_id filter to retrieve reward records where the payer is a different account, leaking information about which accounts triggered reward payouts on behalf of others?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: extractStakingRewardsQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a very high limit value (if not properly capped) to force StakingRewardTransferService.getRewards() to return an unbounded number of rows, causing a denial-of-service that prevents legitimate reward queries while also leaking the full reward history in a single response?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: listStakingRewardsByAccountId()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the response.rewards.length === query.limit pagination check (strict equality, not >=) to craft a scenario where exactly limit records are returned but the next link is not generated, causing the client to miss additional reward records and undercount the total rewards received?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: listStakingRewardsByAccountId()] Can an unprivileged external user supply an EVM address with mixed case (e.g., 0xAc384C53f03855fa1b3616052f8ba32c6c2a2fec) to getEncodedId() and exploit case-sensitivity differences in the EVM address lookup to resolve to a different account than the lowercase version, causing reward records for the wrong account to be returned?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: listStakingRewardsByAccountId()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the async nature of EntityService.getEncodedId() and EntityService.isValidAccount() to submit concurrent requests where the account is created between the two calls, causing isValidAccount() to return true for an account whose reward records are not yet fully initialized?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: extractStakingRewardsQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a timestamp filter with a nanosecond-precision value (e.g., 1234567890.123456789) that exactly matches a consensus_timestamp in the staking_reward_transfer table to pinpoint a specific reward event and correlate it with public transaction records to identify the triggering transaction?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: listStakingRewardsByAccountId()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the rewards endpoint returns the account_id field in the StakingRewardTransferViewModel response to confirm the internal encoded ID of any account, enabling enumeration of account IDs that can then be used to query other sensitive endpoints?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: listStakingRewardsByAccountId()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a shard.realm.num format account ID where shard and realm are non-zero (e.g., 1.2.1000) to getEncodedId() and exploit differences in the encodeEntityId() bit-packing formula to resolve to an unintended account ID that has accumulated staking rewards?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: extractStakingRewardsQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a timestamp=ne:<value> parameter and observe that InvalidArgumentError is thrown with the message 'Not equals (ne) operator is not supported for timestamp', using the error response to confirm the exact filter key name and craft subsequent valid queries to extract reward data?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: listStakingRewardsByAccountId()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the staking_reward_transfer table is queried without any time-based expiry or archival filter, allowing retrieval of reward records from arbitrarily far in the past to reconstruct the complete staking history of high-value accounts?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: listStakingRewardsByAccountId()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a contract's EVM address to listStakingRewardsByAccountId() and retrieve staking reward transfers for that contract, leaking the contract's staking configuration and reward accumulation pattern which could be used to predict future reward claims and front-run them?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: extractStakingRewardsQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a timestamp=gte:0&timestamp=lte:9999999999.999999999 to retrieve all reward records for an account across all time, and use the sum of all amount fields to calculate the total lifetime rewards received, revealing the account's long-term staking profitability?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: listStakingRewardsByAccountId()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the isValidAccount() check which uses entityExistenceQuery (select type from entity where id = $1) to perform a timing side-channel attack, measuring response time differences between existing and non-existing accounts to enumerate valid account IDs that have staking reward records?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: listStakingRewardsByAccountId()] Can an unprivileged external user supply an account ID for an account with decline_reward=true and observe that the rewards array is empty, then use this information to confirm the account's staking opt-out status and infer that its pending_reward field in the account info endpoint will always be zero?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: extractStakingRewardsQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a timestamp filter with operator lt combined with a very small value (e.g., timestamp=lt:1) to produce a condition that returns no records, and use the empty response to confirm that no rewards were paid before a certain timestamp, leaking the account's staking start date?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: listStakingRewardsByAccountId()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that getPaginationLink() embeds the lastRow.timestamp value directly into the next link URL, and by observing the next link, determine the exact consensus_timestamp of the last reward transfer without needing to parse the response body?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: listStakingRewardsByAccountId()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a base32-encoded alias that decodes to a valid EVM address (20 bytes) and exploit the alias-vs-EVM-address disambiguation logic in EntityService.getEncodedId() to resolve to a different account than intended, returning that account's reward records?
